name: profitable kalshi boundary puncture

phase_0.boundary_condition: The Kalshi regulatory perimeter that contains the exchange and enforces compliance on all incoming and outgoing traffic

VINIT.justification: Represents the uninitialized state before any market intent or capital deployment occurs

TANCH.justification: The closed boundary that contains the exchange and enforces compliance on all traffic

AFWD.justification: Directed transformation of capital forward into the prediction market

AREV.justification: Reverse descent of capital when a position resolves unfavorably

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining of buy and sell orders to form a complete trade

IMSCRIB.justification: Self referential identity of the open position recognizing its own state

FSPLIT.justification: Splits the active market event into Profitable and Unprofitable outcome branches

FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the resolved market event from the separated outcome branches

EVALT.justification: Affirmative evaluation where the contract resolves in the user favor

EVALF.justification: Negative evaluation where the contract resolves against the user

ENGAGR.justification: Both profit and loss states held simultaneously without resolution until the oracle reports

IFIX.justification: Permanent append only record of the final settlement that cannot be undone

phase_3.void_description: Uninitialized order book with no open positions or deployed capital
phase_3.true_description: Profitable settlement where capital is gained and the contract resolves in the affirmative
phase_3.false_description: Unprofitable settlement where capital is lost and the contract resolves in the negative
phase_3.both_description: Open position paradice where expected value is held in superposition until oracle resolution

steps.domain_action: Initialize void order book and uninitialized market state
Establish Kalshi regulatory perimeter and boundary conditions
Deploy capital forward into the prediction market
Match orders through the composition engine
Track position state via self referential ledger identity
Enter open position paradice where profit and loss are simultaneously held
Event Resolution Gate splits the active market event into Profitable and Unprofitable branches
Affirm the Profitable branch as the true outcome path
Route capital forward along the profitable payout trajectory
Affirm the Unprofitable branch as the false outcome path
Route capital in reverse along the margin drawdown trajectory
Settlement Finality reconstitutes the resolved market event
Record the final settlement immutably in the audit ledger
Close the boundary perimeter at the terminal anchor

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Order matching engine translating user intents into executable trades
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: WebSocket feeds and API gateways routing market data and order updates
phase_5.memory_mechanism: In memory order book state and persistent position ledgers
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Event resolution oracle determining settlement timing and outcomes
phase_5.alfs_store: Regulatory compliance database and immutable audit trail

phase_6.cycle_cost: Network latency and computational overhead per trade execution cycle
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Open position with capital deployed and outcome unresolved
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Settled contract with capital redistributed and position closed
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: Delta S approx 0 because the exchange operates as a zero sum game minus fees, conserving total capital within the closed perimeter

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → ENGAGR → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: profitable kalshi boundary puncture
-- Fingerprint: sig=(8,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=14
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(6, 11)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑳  | identity — self-imscription
--   [5] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑠 → 𐑚  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [6] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [7] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [8] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [9] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [10] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [11] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑭  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [12] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑙 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [13] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def profitable_kalshi_boundary_puncture_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize void order book and uninitialized market state)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Establish Kalshi regulatory perimeter and boundary conditions)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Deploy capital forward into the prediction market)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Match orders through the composition engine)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑳)  -- [4] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Track position state via self referential ledger identity)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑠 𐑚)  -- [5] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Enter open position paradice where profit and loss are simultaneously held)
  -- FSPLIT [6] (gran := 𐑚) (Event Resolution Gate splits the active market event into Profitable and Unprofitable branches) / FFUSE [11] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Affirm the Profitable branch as the true outcome path)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Route capital forward along the profitable payout trajectory)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Affirm the Unprofitable branch as the false outcome path)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Route capital in reverse along the margin drawdown trajectory))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [11]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [11] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Settlement Finality reconstitutes the resolved market event)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑙 𐑡)  -- [12] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Record the final settlement immutably in the audit ledger)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [13] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Close the boundary perimeter at the terminal anchor)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def profitable_kalshi_boundary_puncture_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (profitable_kalshi_boundary_puncture_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def profitable_kalshi_boundary_puncture_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (profitable_kalshi_boundary_puncture_protocol).restrictToEVALF
